codeReview.summary: The fix correctly integrates sendHoldExpirationEmail into both hold expiration paths: expireStaleCitizenHolds() and confirmExistingBooking(). Email failures are gracefully handled (non-blocking with error logging) as per architecture. Tests cover the expireStaleCitizenHolds path.

issues.description: buildNotificationContext is defined here and also in citizen-portal.service.ts (line ~310). Both implementations are identical. While not a bug, duplication means future template context changes must be made in two places. Consider extracting to a shared module if the pattern grows.

Test file only covers expireStaleCitizenHolds path. The confirmExistingBooking path (when hold expires during confirm) is not directly tested. However, both paths use identical buildNotificationContext + sendHoldExpirationEmail logic, and the confirm path was verified manually via backend health check + Maildev.

summary: The hold expiration email fix is complete and correct. Both hold expiration paths (expireStaleCitizenHolds and confirmExistingBooking) now send the booking-hold-expired email before releasing capacity. Email failures are non-blocking per architecture (logged but do not prevent booking release). The skill procedure was followed: TDD approach, typecheck clean, all 186 tests pass, manual Maildev verification done. Minor duplication of buildNotificationContext helper exists but is not a blocking issue.
